description: §65.6 — reactive theming variant-type inference from `for=`. The bound cell is declared BARE (`<mode> = .Light`); its variant type is INFERRED from the `<theme for=@mode>` binding (§14.10 bare-variant inference — the engine `for=` pattern). The `<theme>` is the single owner of the variant set (`.Light`/`.Dark`), so `<mode> = .Light` does NOT fire E-VARIANT-AMBIGUOUS and the `@mode = .Dark` toggle write resolves against the theme's variant set. Clean compile.
rationale: §65.6: `@mode` is a convention, NOT a reserved name — the adopter names the cell, declared bare, and the variant type flows from `<theme for=@mode>` (§14.10). Because the theme supplies the type context, the bare `.Light` is NOT ambiguous (no E-VARIANT-AMBIGUOUS), and a reassign to a DECLARED variant (`.Dark`) type-checks (no E-TYPE-063). The toggle write also gives `@mode` a consumer so the reactive cell is live. This is the positive half of the bracketing pair with `bare-variant-no-theme-ambiguous`.
